# T3 Code Ultralight Browser Fork

Ultralight localhost bridge, headless client, and embeddable chat UI for a user's authenticated local Codex.

- Machine-readable integration contract: ./integration.json
- One-command verified recipe: run the release CLI with `setup --mode iframe|react|element|custom --json`
- Zero-install browser delivery: add `--delivery hosted` for element or custom modes
- Complete-chat host control: `codex-chat.sendPrompt()`, React imperative ref, or `/codex-embed.js` for raw iframes
- Host commands: send, stop, and newThread; exact parent window and bridge-allowed Origin required; acknowledgements never contain prompt or response text
- Non-loopback hosts: pass exact `--allow-origin`; the receipt repeats it under `originPolicy`
- Trusted file or sandbox hosts: pass `--allow-origin null` and require `opaqueOriginAllowed: true`
- Complete chat budget: 110000 decoded JS+CSS bytes; built-in safe Markdown has no external runtime dependency
- Agent implementation guide: ./docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md
- Human quickstart and API overview: ./README.md
- Detailed recipes and security model: ./docs/INTEGRATION.md
- Public repository: https://github.com/adamholter/t3-code-ultralight-browser-fork

Prefer the complete isolated chat unless the host already owns a custom canvas, voice, spatial, game, or bespoke interface. Keep the bridge loopback-only, preserve approvals, and verify one real Codex turn through the final UI.
